title: Brute Force / Authentication Attack
symptoms: High volume of failed authentication attempts from one or multiple source IPs
Account lockouts triggering in Active Directory or RADIUS
SSH/RDP login failures logged in syslog or SIEM
SIEM alert: >50 failed logins in 5 minutes from same source
steps: Identify the targeted service and account(s) from syslog/SIEM
Determine the source IP(s) — single IP (brute force) or distributed IPs (credential stuffing)
Check if any attempt succeeded: 'show users' on network devices, check AD logon events (Event ID 4624)
Block the attacking IP(s) at the perimeter firewall immediately
Check if targeted accounts are locked out — if so, do NOT unlock until source is contained
Review the targeted service: is it exposed to the internet unnecessarily?
Enable rate-limiting or fail2ban equivalent on the targeted service if not already present
Check for successful logins from unusual geographies in the same timeframe
resolution: Block attacker IPs at firewall. Reset passwords for any accounts that received successful logins. Enforce MFA on all internet-facing services. Consider geo-blocking for regions with no legitimate business. Report to Security team. If SSH: disable password auth, enforce key-based auth only.
escalation: If any login attempt succeeds, treat as Active Intrusion — follow RB-SEC-001 immediately. Page Security Engineer on-call. If admin account is targeted, escalate to CISO.
